chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الصَّائِمِ يَتَقَيَّأُ أَوْ يَذْرَعُهُ الْقَيْ‏ءُ أَوْ يَقْلِسُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 27 – The Fasting one vomits or nauseates the vomit, or regurgitates

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/286)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/286)
text: الَّذِي يَذْرَعُهُ الْقَيْ‏ءُ وَ هُوَ صَائِمٌ قَالَ يُتِمُّ صَوْمَهُ وَ لَا يَقْضِي .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin ShAzan, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muawiya,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the one who nauseates to vomit (but does not), and he is Fasting. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Let him complete his Fast and the does not have to make up for it’.
en.sarwar: 3. Muhammad ibn ‘Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan, from ibn abu ‘Umayr, from Mu‘awiyah who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One who feels like vomiting must complete his fast and there is no Qada’ (compensatory fast) on him.’”
